summary_text: - [Critical] Primary-source recovery is not persisted to registry storage, so review-eligibility depends on ephemeral in-memory metadata only. - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto_quoted_author_sync.py:293-306` sets `primary_source_*` in `metadata`, but `scripts/local/integrations/xauto-quoted-author-sync.sh:347-353` writes only `source_url/normalized_source_url/source_type/source_domain/source_hash` into `quoted_sources`, dropping recovery strategy/confidence and source path. - Failure mode: a run replay or downstream consumer loses recovered non-X lineage and re-qualifies records as X-only-blocked (`scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py:566-575`), destabilizing acceptance. - [Critical] API failure branches are silently swallowed, causing partial/inaccurate datasets with no actionable error signal. - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto_quoted_author_sync.py:235-239` wraps `fetch_tweets_by_ids` in bare `except` and ignores errors. - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto_quoted_author_sync.py:196-200` does the same for per-author timeline fetch fallback. - Failure mode: X API rate/transport errors silently degrade enrichment, pushing more rows into `review_eligibility=blocked` while reporting success. - [High] Domain extraction is not URL-parser safe and can produce missing/incorrect domains into registry analytics and gating logic. - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto-quoted-author-sync.sh:260-263` derives `source_domain` via `awk -F/ '{print $3}'`. - Failure mode: malformed/atypical URLs can produce empty/garbage `source_domain`, weakening future domain-based diversity controls in generator logic (`scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py:483-486`). - [High] Supabase event dedupe is not race-safe due to missing DB-level uniqueness and upsert semantics. - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto-quoted-author-sync.sh:367-381` relies on `NOT EXISTS` + `event_hash`-based insert filtering. - `supabase/migrations/20260406193000_x_auto_quoted_author_registry_v1.sql:41-58` defines no UNIQUE/PK constraint on event fingerprint/context fields. - Failure mode: concurrent/rerun jobs can race to insert duplicate `quote_events`, inflating history and affecting any downstream diversity/selection metrics. - [High] Observability for blocked candidates is too coarse for production tuning. - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py:617-620` emits only `review-eligibility-blocked` for all non-eligible causes. - Failure mode: root causes (missing URL normalization, low-confidence primary resolution, strategy/type failures) are mixed, making diagnosis and remediation of acceptance drop hard in incident response. - [Medium] Candidate scoring path is crash-prone on malformed metadata and can abort generation. - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py:105-110` and `120-123` cast metadata confidence and assume parseable `confidence`; invalid metadata causes `ValueError` during `sorted(records, key=score_record)` in `dedupe_and_select`. - Failure mode: one bad seed row can fail the whole batch and produce zero outputs. - [Medium] Test coverage misses critical persisted-state and failure-safety assertions for registry writer path. - `tests/test-xauto-quoted-author-sync.sh` validates enrichment fields in process output only (`:93-95`) and does not assert SQL payload persistence of `primary_source_*`, or recovery on API exceptions. - `tests/test-xauto-draft-only.sh` checks blocked/rejected presence but not blocked-reason taxonomy fidelity or bridge write outcomes (`:95-103`, `:87-94`), so runtime regressions in registry persistence and smoke/execute error semantics are weakly covered.
